raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "SB 94 allows individuals sentenced to Life Without Parole prior to June 5, 1990, who have served a minimum of 25 years, to have their cases reconsidered for resentencing.",
    "Individuals serving Life Without Parole with special circumstances do not currently have Judicial Review of their original sentences.",
    "The bill creates a four-tiered process allowing Judicial Courts to have complete discretion in resentencing decisions.",
    "Public safety is prioritized in the four-tiered process established by SB 94.",
    "Only individuals who have rehabilitated according to a Judicial Court, the Parole Board, and the Governor may be considered for release under SB 94.",
    "SB 94 does not allow anyone convicted of killing a peace officer, sex offenses, or serial killing to apply for resentencing.",
    "The process established by SB 94 is not new, as the Governor has the power to commute and pardon any sentence at any time."
  ]
}
```
